layman_headline: Crypto market is fearful and alts are bleeding — patience is the play right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a 'Fear' state with most alternative coins (altcoins) dropping 5-20% over the past week while Bitcoin holds relatively steady. This means money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin or to the sidelines. None of the swing trade candidates present a clean, high-conviction setup — most have bearish chart structures, poor risk/reward ratios, or have already bounced significantly. Jumping in now would be catching a falling knife without a clear catalyst to reverse the trend.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines with cash ready. Wait for the Fear & Greed index to bottom and start turning, or for a clear oversold bounce with volume confirmation before entering any new position.
expert_reasoning: Across all 10 candidates, the macro environment is hostile to altcoins: BTC dominance at 56.2% is squeezing alt liquidity, Fear & Greed at 26 suggests capitulation hasn't fully played out, and no volume breakout signals (our most reliable indicator at 100% win rate) are present. TRUMP has the strongest oversold RSI reading at 29.04, which historically signals bounce zones (RSI is 75% reliable in our track record), but the chart bias is -0.70 (bearish), price is below all major MAs, and there's no bullish candlestick confirmation. HTX has the best chart bias (+1.10) with a Bullish Marubozu, but its RSI at 58 suggests it may be overextended, and it's a micro-cap DAO token with minimal liquidity. The Blockworks note about 'ETH derivatives reset' and CoinDesk's observation about Bitcoin nearing power law support suggest we may be near a broader market inflection, but it hasn't triggered yet. Discipline demands we wait for confirmation.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.2% continues to drain altcoin liquidity — any further BTC strength crushes alt positions
Fear & Greed at 26 with only marginal improvement suggests further downside is possible before a true bottom
No volume breakout signals detected across any candidate — our highest-conviction indicator is silent
watch_list: TRUMP RSI dropping below 25 or a bullish reversal candle (hammer/morning star) on elevated volume — would trigger a BUY
Fear & Greed index breaking above 30 with BTC holding $63,000 support — signals risk appetite returning
ZEC momentum (+15.5% 7d) — if it pulls back to support with volume confirmation, could become a strong entry
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear-driven consolidation phase with BTC dominance rising and altcoins broadly selling off. Bitcoin is testing power law support near $63,700 while stablecoin market cap contraction signals reduced overall crypto liquidity.
context_banner: Market fear at 26/100 with rising BTC dominance creates a hostile environment for altcoin entries — discipline requires waiting for volume-confirmed reversals.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 26 (Fear) with only marginal week-over-week improvement from 24 — not yet signaling capitulation bottom, but approaching levels where contrarian entries historically work (80% win rate in our track record).
TRUMP RSI at 29 is the only truly oversold reading among candidates, historically a bounce zone, but lacks confirmation via bullish candle or volume spike.
Bitcoin nearing Fidelity's power law support line tracked since 2015 — if this holds, it could catalyze a broader market bounce including alts.
ETH derivatives reset and DePIN/gaming rebound narrative suggest potential rotation back into alts, but timing remains uncertain.
Zero volume breakout signals detected across all candidates — our most reliable indicator (100% win rate, +13.4% avg return) is completely silent, strongly supporting a HOLD decision.
